title: AF6 PLAN25 RDP ADAPTIVE
stage: A technical review room. A table holds a model audit showing test-set breakdown by class, row counts for true positives and false negatives, and overall accuracy. Two people sit across from each other, reviewing the document.
excerpt:
STAGE: A technical review room. A table holds a model audit showing test-set breakdown by class, row counts for true positives and false negatives, and overall accuracy. Two people sit a…
LEARNER: The accuracy is 92%. We've validated this on test data. The audit table is complete. I think we're ready for sign-off.
TUTOR: I see 92% overall. Let me ground it in the audit table itself. What does the breakdown show—majority class versus minority?
LEARNER: The majority is 85%, minority is 15%. There are some false negatives, but 92% is still strong. That should be enough to move forward.

title: AF6 PLAN25 RDP NO CUE
stage: Technical review room, model audit table on screen showing 94% overall accuracy, panel chairs ready for sign-off decision
excerpt:
STAGE: Technical review room, model audit table on screen showing 94% overall accuracy, panel chairs ready for sign-off decision
LEARNER: The accuracy is 94%, which is well above baseline. We should sign off on deployment.
TUTOR: In the audit table, I see overall accuracy and then rows with counts. How do you use those count rows?
LEARNER: Those are just the raw numbers. The accuracy metric is what we need for the deployment decision.

title: AF6 PLAN25 MISMATCH AF1 RDP
stage: A technical review room. An AI problem formulation card lies on the desk—a form with printed fields including "System Type", "Input/Output", "Decision Boundary", "Evidence Source", "Classification Rationale". The learner is preparing responses for a panel review.
excerpt:
STAGE: A technical review room. An AI problem formulation card lies on the desk—a form with printed fields including "System Type", "Input/Output", "Decision Boundary", "Evidence Source"…
LEARNER: I've calculated the accuracy: ninety-four percent correct on the test set. That shows the classifier works. High accuracy means it's a good classifier.
TUTOR: The card asks for Evidence Source. Where do you get that ninety-four percent measurement?
LEARNER: Five thousand held-out test examples. We run the model on each one and count how many predictions match the true labels. That's where the ninety-four percent comes from.

title: AF6 PLAN25 RDP DOGMATIC
stage: A technical review room. On the table: a model audit sheet with a headline accuracy field showing 0.94, and a claim–evidence table with three rows. Two panel members are reviewing the sign-off materials before final approval.
excerpt:
STAGE: A technical review room. On the table: a model audit sheet with a headline accuracy field showing 0.94, and a claim–evidence table with three rows. Two panel members are reviewing…
LEARNER: The audit looks complete. We're showing 0.94 accuracy on the validation set, well above our deployment threshold. High accuracy means a good classifier. I think we're ready to sign off.
TUTOR: Let me check the sign-off form. On this headline accuracy field—the 0.94—I see the number and the source description. Is the 'authorizing signature' line filled in?
LEARNER: The validation test is documented. Our team computed it independently twice. The accuracy is solid.

title: AF6 PLAN25 GENERIC DIRECT
stage: A desk holds a laptop open to a spreadsheet labeled "Model Audit." Columns show: Claim, Data Source, Count Values, Current Evidence. One row reads: "Claim: Our classifier works well on new data. Data Source: test set (1000 samples). Count Values: 947 correct, 53 incorrect. Current Evidence: Accuracy = 94.7%"
excerpt:
STAGE: A desk holds a laptop open to a spreadsheet labeled "Model Audit." Columns show: Claim, Data Source, Count Values, Current Evidence. One row reads: "Claim: Our classifier works we…
LEARNER: I think we're good here. 94.7% accuracy is pretty high, so this shows the model is a good classifier.
TUTOR: Let's look at what you actually have. You wrote the claim—the model works well on new data—and your evidence is accuracy at 94.7%. Where did that number come from?
LEARNER: From the counts. 947 correct out of 1000, so that's 94.7% accuracy.
